material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Customer Obsession — Leaders start with the customer and work backwards.
text: Tell me about a time when you went above and beyond for a customer or end user.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Ownership — Leaders act on behalf of the entire company, beyond just their own team.
text: Tell me about a time you took on something significant outside your area of responsibility.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Invent and Simplify — Leaders expect and require innovation from their teams and always find ways to simplify.
text: Tell me about a time you found a simple solution to a complex problem.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Are Right, A Lot — Leaders have strong judgment and good instincts.
text: Tell me about a time when you had to make a decision with limited data. How did you arrive at your decision?

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Hire and Develop the Best — Leaders raise the performance bar with every hire and promotion.
text: Tell me about a time you mentored or coached someone. What was the impact?

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Insist on the Highest Standards — Leaders have relentlessly high standards that many people may think are unreasonably high.
text: Tell me about a time when you refused to compromise on quality even when under pressure to deliver.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Bias for Action — Speed matters in business. Many decisions are reversible and do not need extensive study.
text: Tell me about a time when you took a calculated risk and moved forward without having all the answers.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Earn Trust — Leaders listen attentively, speak candidly, and treat others respectfully.
text: Tell me about a time you had to deliver difficult feedback to a peer or manager. How did you handle it?

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Dive Deep — Leaders operate at all levels, stay connected to the details, and audit frequently.
text: Tell me about a time when you identified a problem that others had missed by digging into the details.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Disagree and Commit — Leaders respectfully challenge decisions when they disagree, but once a decision is made, they commit wholly.
text: Tell me about a time when you disagreed with your manager or team on a decision. What did you do?

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Deliver Results — Leaders focus on the key inputs and deliver them with the right quality and in a timely fashion.
text: Tell me about a time when you had to deliver a project under a very tight deadline. How did you ensure success?
Tell me about a time you had to deal with ambiguity on a project. How did you create clarity for your team?
Describe a situation where you had to influence a team or stakeholders without having direct authority over them.
Tell me about your most challenging cross-functional collaboration. What made it difficult and how did you navigate it?
Give me an example of a time you used data to make a decision that others initially disagreed with.
Tell me about a time you had to prioritize between competing demands from different stakeholders.
Describe a time when a project you were leading failed. What happened and what did you learn?
Tell me about a time you had to quickly learn a new technology or domain to solve a problem.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Frugality — Accomplish more with less. Constraints breed resourcefulness, self-sufficiency, and invention.
text: Tell me about a time when you had to achieve a goal with significantly fewer resources than you expected.

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Think Big — Leaders create and communicate a bold direction that inspires results.
text: Tell me about a time when you proposed a bold, unconventional idea. How did you get buy-in?
Tell me about a time you received negative feedback from a customer or user. How did you respond?
Describe a situation where you had to balance speed of delivery with technical quality.
Tell me about the most impactful project you have worked on. What was your specific contribution?

material: Amazon Leadership Principle: Learn and Be Curious — Leaders are never done learning and always seek to improve themselves.
text: Tell me about a time you proactively learned something new that ended up being valuable for your work.
Tell me about a time you had to deal with a significant conflict within your team. How did you resolve it?
Describe a time when you went above and beyond to exceed a customer's expectations, even when it wasn't required.
Tell me about a time you took ownership of a problem that was outside your area of responsibility.
Give an example of when you simplified a complex process or system that others had accepted as is.

material: Amazon LP: Are Right, A Lot
text: Tell me about a time your judgment was challenged by data or peers, and how you responded.

material: Amazon LP: Hire and Develop the Best
text: Describe a situation where you mentored someone and it had a measurable impact on their growth.

material: Amazon LP: Insist on the Highest Standards
text: Tell me about a time you refused to ship something because it didn't meet your quality bar.
Give an example of a calculated risk you took when you had incomplete information.
Describe a time you had to deliver difficult feedback to a colleague or manager. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you discovered a root cause that everyone else had missed by digging into the details.

material: Amazon LP: Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit
text: Describe a situation where you disagreed with your manager's decision but ultimately committed to it.
Tell me about a time you delivered an important project under a very tight deadline.
Give an example of when you accomplished a significant result with fewer resources than expected.
Describe a bold idea you proposed that changed the direction of a project or team.

material: Amazon LP: Learn and Be Curious
text: Tell me about the last thing you learned on your own that you later applied at work.

material: Amazon LP: Strive to be Earth's Best Employer
text: Describe a time you advocated for a better working environment or process for your teammates.

material: Amazon LP: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
text: Tell me about a time you considered the broader societal or ethical impact of a technical decision.
Tell me about a time you helped create an inclusive environment on your team.
Describe a time you had to learn a domain you knew nothing about to solve a problem. Walk me through your approach.
Tell me about a technical design decision you made that you would approach differently today, and why.
Describe a time you navigated a situation where your team was resistant to change.
Tell me about the most complex problem you've debugged. How did you break it down?
Tell me about a time you shipped something quickly and iterated based on real-world feedback.
Describe a time you proposed an unconventional solution that others were initially skeptical about.
Tell me about a decision where you sacrificed short-term gains for long-term value.
Describe a product or feature you're most proud of building. What made it great?
Tell me about a time you changed your mind after receiving critical feedback on your work.
Describe a professional setback that taught you something valuable. How did you apply that lesson?
Tell me about a time you used customer insights to pivot the direction of a project.
Describe a situation where you worked with someone whose perspective was very different from yours. What did you learn?
Tell me about a time you broke down silos between teams to achieve a shared goal.
Describe a time your work had a meaningful impact beyond your immediate team or product.
Tell me about a time a small detail you caught prevented a significant issue downstream.
Describe a time you challenged the status quo to improve a product or user experience.
Tell me about a time you worked closely with a designer, PM, or non-engineering stakeholder to ship a better product.
Describe a time you made a high-stakes decision independently, without waiting for approval.
Tell me about a time you empowered a teammate by sharing context rather than giving directives.
Describe a situation where you gave radically candid feedback to a high-performing peer.
Tell me about a time you had to onboard onto a legacy codebase with poor documentation. How did you become productive?
Describe a situation where you had to say no to a request from a senior leader. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you identified and resolved a performance bottleneck in a production system.
Describe a time you had to manage competing priorities with no clear guidance on what to do first.
Tell me about a time you built consensus among people who had strongly opposing views.
Describe a situation where you proactively identified a risk before it became a problem.
Tell me about a time you had to communicate a highly technical concept to a non-technical audience.
Describe a time you received vague or shifting requirements. How did you move forward?
Tell me about a time you had to make a trade-off between technical debt and feature delivery.
Describe a situation where you automated a tedious manual process. What was the impact?
Tell me about a time you dealt with a production outage or incident. What was your role and what did you learn?
Describe a time you had to adapt your communication style to work effectively with a difficult stakeholder.
Tell me about a project where scope kept expanding. How did you manage it?
Describe a time you had to rapidly reprioritize your work due to an unexpected change in business needs.
Tell me about a time you improved the developer experience or internal tooling for your team.
Describe a situation where you had to balance the needs of multiple teams with conflicting timelines.
Tell me about a time you led a migration or large-scale refactor. How did you plan and execute it?
Describe a time you had to work with incomplete or unreliable data to make a recommendation.
Tell me about a time you championed an unpopular idea that ultimately proved successful.
Describe a situation where you had to recover trust with a teammate or stakeholder after a mistake.
Tell me about a time you successfully transferred knowledge to ensure continuity when leaving a project or team.
Describe a time you scaled a system or process to handle significantly more load or users than originally designed.
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a product requirement and what you did about it.
